events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON only. Need analyze scenario: residential job, conductor selection & sizing, observed field condition: aluminum on device not rated CO/ALR. Work rough-complete. Need estimate/interpret measurable condition and state why out of tolerance.

We need decide pass/fail. Aluminum conductors connected to device terminals not rated CO/ALR is non-compliant and high risk. Decision fail. Risk high or critical? Aluminum on non-CO/ALR device can cause loose connections, overheating, fire. In residential, critical maybe. But risk categories: low/medium/high/critical. Use high or critical. Since not yet energized maybe high. But potential fire hazard; critical. However component condition installed-defective and non-compliant. Percent complete rough-complete maybe 80. Value? Could be numeric reading not asked; maybe 0. But scenario says measurable cue visible enough to estimate whether condition within tolerance. We can set value 0 (not a numeric measurement). Or maybe number of defects? The schema requires value numeric. Use 0. Confidence 0.95. Event_time 0, rate 0, order empty, sound_source empty, workable empty. Findings: aluminum conductor terminated on device terminal not listed/rated CO/ALR; incompatible material causing potential overheating/fire. Actions: replace device with CO/ALR-rated device or use approved alumiconn connectors/pigtail; ensure termination torque and anti-oxidant. Rationale: NEC requires terminals/conectors used with aluminum conductors be listed and marked CO/ALR (or equivalent) to prevent loosening and overheating; device without marking is not rated for aluminum. References: NEC 110.3(B), 110.14, 406.2(C) maybe, Table 310.16 for conductor ampacity. But prompt says reference material NEC 310, Table 310.16. Need include those. Also maybe NEC 110.3(B) and 110.14. Let's craft JSON.

Need ensure no prose outside JSON.
